1845781.title: Steven Wilson
1845781.extract:
Steven John Wilson (born 3 November 1967) is an English musician. He is most associated with the progressive rock genre, though his influences and work extend beyond it. Wilson first became known as the founder, lead guitarist, singer, and songwriter of the band Porcupine Tree, as well as being a member of several other bands.

Wilson has been involved in musical projects including No-Man, Bass Communion, Blackfield, and Storm Corrosion. He has also worked as a record producer and is known for his surround sound and high-fidelity stereo remixes of classic albums by artists such as King Crimson, Jethro Tull, Tears for Fears, Roxy Music, and Yes.

His solo career began in earnest in 2008. He has released six solo studio albums, including The Raven That Refused to Sing (And Other Stories) (2013) and Hand. Cannot. Erase. (2015), both of which received critical acclaim. His seventh album, The Overview, was released in 2025.
